proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rates (negative effect on response).
Female sex (sex_female==1) is associated with a different objective_response rate than male sex.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate falls monotonically with ECOG PS: 23.10% at ECOG 0 (n=17592), 17.43% at ECOG 1 (n=24971), 12.91% at ECOG 2 (n=7437). Chi-square p=1.79e-90.
Response rate 18.91% in females (n=22371) vs 18.62% in males (n=27629). Chi-square p=0.42.
Univariable logistic: age beta = -0.0002 per year (OR=0.9998), p=0.882. No detectable main effect of age.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation==1 have higher objective_response rates than NPM1-wildtype patients.
Patients with tp53_mutation==1 have lower objective_response rates than TP53-wildtype patients.
Patients with complex_karyotype==1 have lower objective_response rates than those with complex_karyotype==0.
FLT3-ITD (flt3_itd) main effect on objective_response is non-zero.
FLT3-TKD (flt3_tkd), idh1_mutation, idh2_mutation each have non-zero main effects on objective_response.
secondary_aml is associated with lower objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1-mutated response 23.46% (n=15014) vs NPM1-WT 16.73% (n=34986). Diff +6.74 pp, chi-square p=5.79e-70.
TP53-mutated response 16.57% (n=4985) vs TP53-WT 18.99% (n=45015). Diff -2.42 pp, chi-square p=3.51e-05.
Complex karyotype response 16.81% (n=7462) vs non-complex 19.09% (n=42538). Diff -2.29 pp, chi-square p=3.31e-06.
FLT3-ITD+ response 18.92% (n=10000) vs FLT3-ITD- 18.71% (n=40000). Diff +0.21 pp, p=0.636. No main effect.
All near-null: FLT3-TKD diff -0.75 pp p=0.337; IDH1 diff +0.37 pp p=0.596; IDH2 diff +0.44 pp p=0.474.
Secondary-AML response 18.81% (n=12301) vs de-novo 18.73% (n=37699). Diff +0.08 pp, p=0.851. No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow predicts lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl predicts higher objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo predicts lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul predicts lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l predicts lower objective_response.
Hemoglobin, LDH, NLR, and other lab markers have no detectable association with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: beta = -0.0049 per % blasts (OR=0.9951), p=2.41e-16. Higher marrow blast burden lowers response.
beta = +0.1175 per g/dL (OR=1.125), p=2.86e-07. Higher albumin associates with higher response.
beta = -0.0357 per percentage point of weight loss (OR=0.965), p=1.14e-30. Strong negative effect.
beta = -0.0018 per K/uL (OR=0.998), p=1.85e-04. Higher WBC slightly lowers response.
beta = -0.0064 per mg/L (OR=0.994), p=6.05e-06. Inflammation marker negatively associates.

analyses.code: univariable Logit for hemoglobin, ldh, nlr, ast, alt, total_bilirubin, creatinine, bun, sodium, potassium, calcium, alkaline_phosphatase
analyses.result_summary: None reach p<0.05 except alkaline_phosphatase borderline (p=0.051). hemoglobin p=0.150, ldh p=0.350, nlr p=0.919, ast p=0.128, alt p=0.195, total_bilirubin p=0.216, creatinine p=0.716, bun p=0.548, sodium p=0.616, potassium p=0.764, calcium p=0.813.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response rate compared with patients not receiving it.
treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response rate compared with patients not receiving it.
treatment_midostaurin and treatment_gilteritinib (FLT3 inhibitors) each increase objective_response rate overall.
treatment_ivosidenib and treatment_enasidenib (IDH inhibitors) each increase objective_response rate overall.
unfit_for_intensive==1 patients have higher objective_response rates than unfit_for_intensive==0 patients (paradoxical association driven by treatment selection).

analyses.result_summary: Ven/aza response 21.96% (n=20020) vs no-ven/aza 16.61% (n=29980). Diff +5.35 pp, chi-square p=6.62e-51.
7+3 response 18.89% (n=22580) vs no-7+3 18.63% (n=27420). Diff +0.26 pp, p=0.465. No overall main effect.
Midostaurin: 18.57% (n=7545) vs 18.78% (n=42455), diff -0.21 pp, p=0.673. Gilteritinib: 18.13% vs 18.86%, diff -0.73 pp, p=0.139. Neither shows a main effect.
Ivosidenib: 18.82% (n=3938) vs 18.74% (n=46062), diff +0.07 pp, p=0.928. Enasidenib: 18.58% (n=3972) vs 18.76% (n=46028), diff -0.18 pp, p=0.791. Neither shows a main effect.
Unfit response 22.10% (n=20056) vs fit 16.51% (n=29944). Diff +5.59 pp, chi-square p=1.88e-55. Counter-intuitive direction; likely driven by selective ven/aza assignment in the unfit group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin's effect on objective_response is more positive in flt3_itd==1 patients than in flt3_itd==0 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_gilteritinib's effect on objective_response is more positive in flt3_itd==1 patients than in flt3_itd==0 patients.
treatment_midostaurin and treatment_gilteritinib have positive effects in flt3_tkd==1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: In FLT3-ITD+: midostaurin 17.64% (n=1468) vs no-midostaurin 19.14% (n=8532), diff -1.50 pp. In FLT3-ITD-: +0.10 pp. Interaction beta=-0.106, p=0.195. Direction opposite to expected; not significant.
In FLT3-ITD+: gilteritinib 19.11% (n=1491) vs 18.89% (n=8509), diff +0.23 pp. In FLT3-ITD-: -0.96 pp. Interaction beta=+0.079, p=0.323. Trend in expected direction but small and not significant.
Midostaurin x FLT3-TKD: in TKD+ diff -0.52 pp; interaction beta=-0.023, p=0.870. Gilteritinib x FLT3-TKD: in TKD+ diff -3.49 pp; interaction beta=-0.213, p=0.156. No support for benefit in FLT3-TKD.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_ivosidenib's effect on objective_response is more positive in idh1_mutation==1 patients than IDH1-WT (positive interaction).
treatment_enasidenib's effect on objective_response is more positive in idh2_mutation==1 patients than IDH2-WT.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response, [const, t, m, t*m]) for ivosidenib x idh1
analyses.result_summary: In IDH1+: ivosidenib 15.44% (n=298) vs no-ivosidenib 19.42% (n=3326), diff -3.99 pp. In IDH1-: +0.40 pp. Interaction beta=-0.304, p=0.077. Trend is OPPOSITE to expected; ivosidenib does not appear to help IDH1-mutated patients in this cohort.

analyses.code: Logit with t*m for enasidenib x idh2
analyses.result_summary: In IDH2+: enasidenib 19.49% (n=354) vs 19.12% (n=4325), diff +0.37 pp. In IDH2-: -0.24 pp. Interaction beta=+0.039, p=0.788. No meaningful targeted-drug-by-mutation enhancement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in unfit_for_intensive==1 patients than in unfit_for_intensive==0 patients (positive interaction).

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response, [const, t, m, t*m]) with t=ven/aza, m=unfit
analyses.result_summary: In unfit==1: ven/aza 30.81% (n=8013) vs 16.30% (n=12043), diff +14.51 pp. In unfit==0: 16.05% vs 16.81%, diff -0.77 pp. Interaction beta=+0.883, p=1.14e-78. Massive heterogeneity: ven/aza benefit confined to the unfit-for-intensive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in npm1_mutation==1 patients than in NPM1-WT patients.

analyses.code: Logit with t*m for ven/aza x npm1
analyses.result_summary: In NPM1+: ven/aza 34.66% (n=6025) vs 15.96% (n=8989), diff +18.69 pp. In NPM1-: 16.49% vs 16.88%, diff -0.39 pp. Interaction beta=+1.055, p=5.50e-102. Very strong positive interaction — NPM1 status is the dominant single modifier of ven/aza response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine's effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in tp53_mutation==1 patients than in TP53-WT patients (negative interaction).

analyses.code: Logit with t*m for ven/aza x tp53
analyses.result_summary: In TP53+: ven/aza 16.36% (n=2048) vs 16.72% (n=2937), diff -0.36 pp. In TP53-WT: 22.60% vs 16.60%, diff +6.00 pp. Interaction beta=-0.409, p=4.67e-07. TP53 mutation completely abolishes ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine's effect on objective_response is smaller in complex_karyotype==1 patients than non-complex (negative interaction).

analyses.code: Logit with t*m for ven/aza x complex_karyotype
analyses.result_summary: In CK+: ven/aza 17.49% (n=3007) vs 16.34% (n=4455), diff +1.15 pp. In CK-: 22.75% vs 16.65%, diff +6.09 pp. Interaction beta=-0.306, p=6.00e-06. Complex karyotype substantially attenuates ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 has a worse effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype==1 patients than non-complex (negative interaction).
treatment_7plus3 has heterogeneous effects across NPM1, TP53, FLT3-ITD, and unfit subgroups.

analyses.code: Logit with t*m for 7+3 x complex_karyotype
analyses.result_summary: In CK+: 7+3 15.55% (n=3415) vs 17.87% (n=4047), diff -2.32 pp. In CK-: +0.72 pp. Interaction beta=-0.213, p=1.51e-03. 7+3 modestly underperforms in complex karyotype.

analyses.code: systematic Logit interaction for 7+3 with each binary feature
analyses.result_summary: Other 7+3 interactions are non-significant after Bonferroni: NPM1 beta=-0.075 p=0.121, TP53 beta=-0.045 p=0.572, FLT3-ITD beta=+0.033 p=0.568, unfit beta=-0.027 p=0.556, secondary_aml beta=-0.078 p=0.146, IDH1 beta=+0.128 p=0.146, IDH2 beta=-0.043 p=0.586. Complex karyotype is the only meaningful 7+3 modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients who are simultaneously npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a much larger increase in objective_response than in any other combination of these two markers.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ & unfit: ven/aza 63.31% (n=2401) vs 15.96% (n=3585), diff +47.35 pp, p=1.89e-275. NPM1+ & fit: 15.67% vs 15.97%, diff -0.30 pp, p=0.705. NPM1- & unfit: 16.91% vs 16.45%, diff +0.46 pp, p=0.469. NPM1- & fit: 16.21% vs 17.18%, diff -0.97 pp, p=0.067. Effect dramatically concentrated in NPM1+ unfit cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients who are npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 AND tp53_mutation==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an even larger increase in objective_response than in the broader NPM1+ unfit subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ & unfit & TP53-WT: ven/aza 69.08% (n=2125) vs 15.69% (n=3224), diff +53.39 pp, p=2.75e-297. NPM1+ & TP53-WT (any fitness): ven/aza 36.70% vs 15.95%, diff +20.75 pp, p=5.45e-160. Excluding TP53+ sharpens the responder definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients who are npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an even larger increase in objective_response than the same subgroup with complex karyotype mixed in.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ & unfit & TP53-WT & non-CK (n=4531, ~9.1% of cohort): ven/aza 78.60% (n=1790) vs 15.83% (n=2741), diff +62.77 pp, chi-square p<1e-300. Outside this subgroup: ven/aza 16.40% (n=18230) vs 16.69% (n=27239), diff -0.29 pp. Cleanest responder definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 subgroup, the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response remains strongly positive after adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, tp53_mutation, and complex_karyotype.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ ven_aza + age + ecog_ps + tp53 + complex_karyotype) within NPM1+ unfit (n=5986)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ven/aza beta = +2.439 (OR ~ 11.5), p<1e-300; ECOG beta = -0.405, p=2.6e-16; TP53 beta = -1.388, p=1.6e-30; complex_karyotype beta = -1.594, p=2.8e-50; age beta = -0.003, p=0.316. Treatment effect is robust after adjustment; TP53 and complex_karyotype attenuate even within the responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is modified by continuous biomarkers (age, blast %, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin, WBC).

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ t + c + t*c) for each continuous c (z-scored)
analyses.result_summary: Only significant continuous modifier: blast_pct_marrow interaction beta=+0.068, p=0.0035 (greater ven/aza benefit at higher marrow blast %). Borderline: weight_loss_pct_6mo beta=+0.050, p=0.039. Not significant: age p=0.50, wbc p=0.083, albumin p=0.51, ldh p=0.51, crp p=0.51, nlr p=0.40, hemoglobin p=0.73. No continuous lab marker is a strong modifier; the categorical mutation/fitness landscape carries the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enasidenib has a more positive effect on objective_response in females (sex_female==1) than males (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: In males: enasidenib 16.48% (n=2191) vs 18.81% (n=25438), diff -2.33 pp. In females: 21.17% vs 18.71%, diff +2.45 pp. Interaction beta=+0.315, p=2.23e-04. Suggestive sex-by-enasidenib interaction; not directly explainable by IDH2 status (three-way enasidenib*sex*IDH2 interaction p=0.616). Likely a chance finding given multiplicity (60+ tests run); flagged for replication.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The absolute increase in objective_response from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine differs across ecog_ps strata.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: ven/aza 26.68% vs 20.72%, diff +5.97 pp. ECOG 1: 20.51% vs 15.38%, diff +5.13 pp. ECOG 2: 15.80% vs 10.91%, diff +4.89 pp. Absolute benefit decreases somewhat with worse ECOG but all strata benefit; ECOG is not the modifier driving the responder phenotype (NPM1 + unfit are).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In tp53_mutation==1 patients, no available treatment (ven/aza, 7+3, midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) produces a meaningful increase in objective_response.

analyses.code: For each treatment, response rate by treatment within TP53+ (n=4985)
analyses.result_summary: Within TP53+ (n=4985): ven/aza 16.36% vs 16.72% (diff -0.36 pp); 7+3 16.38% vs 16.72% (diff -0.34 pp); midostaurin 16.80% vs 16.53% (+0.27 pp); gilteritinib 17.02% vs 16.49% (+0.53 pp); ivosidenib 16.63% vs 16.56% (+0.06 pp); enasidenib 14.56% vs 16.75% (-2.20 pp). All differences within ~2 pp; none statistically significant. TP53 mutation marks treatment refractoriness in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 60 (6 treatments x 10 binary features) treatment-by-feature interaction tests on objective_response, only ven/aza interactions and 7+3 x complex_karyotype survive multiple-testing correction.

analyses.code: All 60 Logit interactions, sorted by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Bonferroni threshold at 60 tests = 0.05/60 = 8.3e-04. Surviving: ven/aza x NPM1 (beta=+1.055, p=5.5e-102), ven/aza x unfit (beta=+0.883, p=1.1e-78), ven/aza x TP53 (beta=-0.409, p=4.7e-07), ven/aza x complex_karyotype (beta=-0.306, p=6.0e-06), enasidenib x sex_female (beta=+0.315, p=2.2e-04), 7+3 x complex_karyotype (beta=-0.213, p=1.5e-03 - fails Bonferroni at 8.3e-04 but is the strongest non-ven/aza signal). FLT3 inhibitor x FLT3 mutation and IDH inhibitor x IDH mutation interactions all p>0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists a subgroup of idh1_mutation==1 patients (defined by additional clinical features) in which treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response.

analyses.code: Stratify ivosidenib effect within IDH1+ across (unfit, ck, tp53, npm1) cells
analyses.result_summary: All IDH1+ subgroups examined: unfit=0/ck=0/tp53=0/npm1=0 diff=-4.87 pp; unfit=0/ck=0/tp53=0/npm1=1 diff=+1.98 pp (n_t=28); unfit=1/ck=0/tp53=0/npm1=0 diff=-1.19 pp; unfit=1/ck=0/tp53=0/npm1=1 diff=-12.39 pp (n_t=25). No reproducible IDH1+ subgroup shows a positive ivosidenib effect; small positive cells have tiny sample sizes. Ivosidenib effect heterogeneity is not clinically interpretable in these data.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: among patients with npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately +63 percentage points (78.6% on treatment vs 15.8% off treatment); outside this subgroup the effect is essentially null.

analyses.code: Final subgroup test, ven/aza effect inside vs outside (NPM1+ & unfit & TP53-WT & non-CK)
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=4531, 9.1% of cohort): ven/aza 78.60% vs 15.83%, diff +62.77 pp. Outside subgroup (n=45469, 90.9%): ven/aza 16.40% vs 16.69%, diff -0.29 pp. Confirmed: the ven/aza benefit is fully concentrated in this 4-feature responder subgroup. The 'unfavorable' modifiers tp53_mutation==1 and complex_karyotype==1 each suppress the effect even in NPM1+ unfit patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_7plus3: 7+3 has near-zero effect on objective_response overall (+0.26 pp); the only meaningful heterogeneity is a modest negative interaction with complex_karyotype (within complex_karyotype==1, 7+3 lowers response by ~2.3 pp; within complex_karyotype==0, +0.7 pp). No subgroup with substantial positive 7+3 benefit was found.

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_midostaurin and treatment_gilteritinib: neither FLT3 inhibitor produces a significant increase in objective_response in any FLT3-ITD or FLT3-TKD subgroup tested. No responder subgroup identified.

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_ivosidenib: in idh1_mutation==1 patients, ivosidenib's directional effect is weakly NEGATIVE (diff ~-4 pp; interaction p=0.077). No identified responder subgroup.

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_enasidenib: there is no benefit in idh2_mutation==1 patients; the only nominally significant interaction is enasidenib x sex_female (beta +0.315, p=2.2e-04), with female enasidenib-treated response 21.2% vs male 16.5%; this is plausibly multiplicity-driven and should be replicated.

analyses.result_summary: 7+3 overall: +0.26 pp, p=0.465. 7+3 x complex_karyotype interaction: beta=-0.213, p=1.51e-03. Within CK+: 7+3 reduces response by 2.32 pp; within CK-: +0.72 pp. No responder subgroup found.

analyses.code: Logit interactions for midostaurin, gilteritinib x flt3 markers
analyses.result_summary: Midostaurin x FLT3-ITD interaction p=0.195; in FLT3-ITD+ subgroup midostaurin reduces response by 1.50 pp. Gilteritinib x FLT3-ITD p=0.323; in FLT3-ITD+ gilteritinib raises by only 0.23 pp. No FLT3 inhibitor responder subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratified ivosidenib effect in IDH1+ across all combinations of unfit/CK/TP53/NPM1
analyses.result_summary: Ivosidenib in IDH1+: -3.99 pp; interaction p=0.077. No subset yields a positive, statistically supported ivosidenib effect with reasonable sample size.

analyses.code: Logit with t*sex for enasidenib; t*sex*idh2 three-way
analyses.result_summary: Enasidenib x sex_female main interaction p=2.2e-04 (suggestive); three-way enasidenib*sex_female*IDH2 p=0.616. Possible chance finding among ~60 interaction tests. No biologically grounded responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within NPM1+ unfit patients (the ven/aza responder population), tp53_mutation==1 and complex_karyotype==1 each independently suppress the ven/aza response (resistance markers).

analyses.code: Within NPM1+ unfit (n=5986) compute ven/aza response by TP53 and CK status
analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ & unfit & TP53-WT & non-CK (n=4531): ven/aza 78.60% vs 15.83%, diff +62.77 pp. Within NPM1+ unfit, dropping to TP53+: response on ven/aza falls to ~17%. Within NPM1+ unfit, dropping to CK+: ven/aza response also falls substantially. In adjusted logit, TP53+ beta=-1.39 (p<1e-30) and CK+ beta=-1.59 (p<1e-50); their negative effects on response within the responder group are independent and roughly additive on the log-odds scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all six recorded treatments, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is the ONLY drug in this cohort with a clinically meaningful, statistically robust subgroup of patients showing a large increase in objective_response; that subgroup is fully described by the simultaneous predicates npm1_mutation==1, unfit_for_intensive==1, tp53_mutation==0, complex_karyotype==0.

analyses.code: Combine all prior subgroup tests; compare best subgroup effect across the 6 treatments.
analyses.result_summary: Best subgroup effect by drug (response-rate diff vs untreated within subgroup): ven/aza in NPM1+ unfit TP53-WT non-CK = +62.77 pp (p<1e-300); 7+3 best = +0.72 pp (CK-) ns; midostaurin best ~ 0; gilteritinib best ~ -0.7 pp; ivosidenib best in IDH1+ = -3.99 pp; enasidenib best ~ +2.5 pp (sex_female). Only ven/aza shows a clinically usable predictive subgroup. Negative modifiers (TP53 mutation, complex karyotype) act as resistance markers that should be combined with positive markers (NPM1+ AND unfit) to define eligibility.
